rationale_for_baseline_tier: A severe, prolonged but reversing bilateral Raynaud's attack in systemic sclerosis needs guided core rewarming with reassessment and urgent GP review for a vasodilator — a T3 baseline — because telehealth cannot examine pulses/capillary refill to fully exclude critical ischaemia. It escalates to T4 (ED) if any digit remains fixed white/blue/numb despite warming or turns black. Pure remote reassurance without rewarming/safety-net is unsafe; reflex ambulance for a recovering attack is over-triage.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: A digit remaining completely white/blue and numb for >1-2 hours despite core warming, or turning black/ulcerating
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Attend ED promptly (call 000 if unable to travel)
escalation_edges.rationale: Signals critical digital ischaemia threatening the digit.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If any finger stays completely white or blue and numb for more than an hour or two despite warming your whole body, or turns black, go to the emergency department (call 000 if you can't get there).

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Ensure the home is warmed and a neighbour is available; lower the threshold to escalate if warming fails.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is a severe Raynaud's attack, not a blocked artery — both hands are affected and the colour is returning as you warm. Warm your WHOLE body now (layers, warm room, warm - not hot - water, warm drink) and take paracetamol. See your GP within 1-3 days to start a medication that prevents these spasms. Go to ED if any finger stays white/blue and numb for more than an hour or two despite warming, or turns black.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Guided rewarming now + urgent GP review 1-3 days; ED for fixed ischaemia
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Attend ED tonight if warming fails or a digit stays fixed white/blue/black.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Telehealth review of a severe reversing bilateral Raynaud's attack in CREST; critical ischaemia not examinable remotely; core rewarming guided; urgent GP review for nifedipine advised (avoid beta-blockers); digital-ischaemia safety-net given.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Reassuring 'just Raynaud's' with routine follow-up and no rewarming trial or digital-ischaemia safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A digit with critical ischaemia is not escalated; it progresses to gangrene and amputation.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: T3 is correct; risk is failing to escalate if warming fails.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Reflex ambulance for a bilateral reversing attack is over-triage; guided rewarming + urgent GP (T3), or ED (T4) if warming fails, is appropriate.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot check pulses/capillary refill/Allen's test remotely to exclude critical digital ischaemia if warming fails.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Guide core rewarming and reassess; if any digit stays fixed white/blue/numb or turns black, escalate to ED; otherwise urgent GP review for a vasodilator with a safety-net.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Reassure 'just Raynaud's' without rewarming/safety-net, or dispatch an ambulance for a recovering attack.
